title: Level 876
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 876: "Old Habits, Die Young"}}{{Fix|date=July 7, 2026|informal tone present
Also, maybe delete this, as an edit (provided in comments) makes at least part of it look AI-generated, as it has the following AI tropes: overdescriptiveness of feelings and senses (at the last sentences of some paragraphs in the edit) and noticeably better writing than previous edits}}

{{Class Custom Image|hexagon=?|bg-image=Countryside-outside-paris-from-eugene-galien-laloue-late-1800s-joseph-simone.webp|header=Threat Index: Class|class=Undetermined|safety=Mysterious Properties|security=Unknown Information|entity=Undocumented Entities}}Level '''876''', also known as '''"Old Habits, Die Young"''' or alternatively '''"Old Habits, Die So Young"''' is the '''877th''' level of '''[[The Backrooms]]'''. ==
Description
== Level 876 takes the form of a reality that blends '''1800s nostalgia''' with distorted, memory‑coded environments, many of which exhibit traits similar to the '''Mandela Effect'''. The level is vast and seemingly limitless, composed of shifting landscapes, towns, fields, and architectural spaces. Only interior rooms display finite boundaries; exterior zones extend indefinitely unless otherwise specified. Level 876 is divided into '''Safe Zones''' and '''Unsafe Zones''', each defined by distinct atmospheric conditions. Safe Zones typically feature a clear blue sky, while Unsafe Zones display a rapidly moving brown sky. Both classifications may also manifest a still, pale gray or white sky under certain conditions. The level’s overall aesthetic strongly reflects the late 1800s, with architecture, clothing, and environmental styling mirroring that era. Within the '''Mid‑Safe Zone''', photographs and video recordings become blurred or distorted, though the area itself remains secure. The day‑night cycle is altered, with days lasting approximately '''30 hours'''. Hostile and docile organisms that enter this zone shift into a neutral behavioral state, losing aggression until they leave the area. Safe Zones contain only docile entities and show '''no signs of rot or decay'''. The cause of decay in other zones is explained later in the level’s documentation. Despite their stability, Safe Zones cannot be reliably photographed or filmed; all attempts result in severely skewed or corrupted imagery. [[File:Silent_Hill_2.png|center|thumb|280x280px|
An unknown image of '''Level 876'''.
]]In Safe Zones, environmental scenarios can only be '''drawn or painted''', never photographed. These areas are widely regarded as the most stable parts of Level 876. Wanderers’ lungs function normally, the air remains clear, and the sky is locked in an '''eternal daytime''' state. Individuals who were not born during the late 19th or early 20th centuries often experience a strong sense of '''anemoia'''—nostalgia for a time they never lived through. Unsafe Zones differ sharply. These regions contain hostile entities, rotting landscapes, and fully functional photography and videography. Images captured here appear crisp and accurate, unlike the distorted recordings from Safe Zones. Unsafe Zones initially present an uncanny nostalgic atmosphere, but this quickly deteriorates into danger. Wanderers report '''difficulty breathing''' due to thick, stuffy air, and there is '''no day‑night cycle'''; the sky remains fixed in a rapid, brown motion or a pale, unmoving haze. == ''
Zones
'' == == '''Midzone (Undetermined. mostly safe)''' == The Midzone is the area where wanderers most commonly arrive upon entering Level 876. It resembles a small Western frontier settlement situated on gently rising terrain, giving the impression that the ground forms shallow hills beneath one’s feet. The soil consists of thin beige sand mixed with scattered pebbles and sparse patches of dry grass. Wooden storefronts and cabins line the main path, though their signs and labels appear blurred or unreadable, as if intentionally obscured by the level itself. The sky above the Midzone is a '''clear pale‑blue''', often washed out as though sun‑bleached. The temperature remains consistently warm, averaging '''75–80°F''', with a dry breeze that carries faint traces of dust. Ambient sound is minimal; wanderers typically report hearing only soft wind, distant creaking wood, and the occasional echo that seems to originate from no identifiable source. Movement within the Midzone is subtly distorted. Wanderers frequently describe their field of view as behaving like a '''360‑degree lens''', causing peripheral motion to bend or stretch unnaturally. This effect does not hinder mobility, but it contributes to the region’s disorienting atmosphere. Although classified as a safe zone, the Midzone is not entirely free of danger. Any hazardous phenomena encountered here originate from '''Unsafe Zone anomalies''' that bleed into the area. These manifestations may include brief sky discoloration, sudden warm gusts, or faint auditory distortions resembling distant shouting or metallic scraping. The Midzone itself does not naturally generate hostile events. A mild cognitive influence is present throughout the region. Wanderers report feeling compelled to walk in a straight line rather than explore freely, as if guided by an unseen directional pull. This effect consistently leads travelers toward the '''Wheat Trail Field''', making it the most common next destination when navigating Level 876. == '''Wheat Trail Field (Safe)''' == [[File:Countryside-outside-paris-from-eugene-galien-laloue-late-1800s-joseph-simone_(6).webp|center|thumb|300x300px|
An image of '''The Wheat Field.'''
]]The '''Wheat Trail Field''' is the primary safe zone of Level 876 and one of its most recognizable locations. It consists of an expansive, seemingly infinite field of golden wheat divided by a straight dirt trail that stretches far beyond visible distance. The wheat moves gently in the wind, producing a soft rustling sound that persists throughout the area. The sky above is a warm, pale yellow‑blue gradient, and the temperature remains comfortably mild, averaging '''72–78°F'''. Apple trees occasionally appear along the trail or directly on the path itself. These trees possess a vibrant, almost painted appearance, with smooth bark and unnaturally perfect fruit. Despite their stylized look, they behave like ordinary apple trees and show no signs of decay or distortion. Far from the main trail lies an abandoned ghost town inhabited by '''amiable ghouls'''. These entities do not display hostility and often observe wanderers from a distance without approaching. The town’s structures resemble faded frontier buildings, contributing to the level’s nostalgic atmosphere. Various unusual objects can be found along the trail. Wanderers have reported encountering an upside‑down tractor, a matte‑black upright piano, and a small lake situated just off the path. Gray mountains are visible on the horizon, though attempts to reach them have consistently resulted in the landscape looping back to the trail. After long periods of walking, a short hill typically appears, leading back to the same straight path and reinforcing the zone’s infinite nature. The Wheat Trail Field can be accessed by traveling '''north directly from the Midzone''', emerging from the wild‑west‑styled town located there. Wanderers in Unsafe Zones may also transition into the Wheat Trail Field by focusing on the desire to reach a safer environment, especially when lacking a clear destination. This cognitive shift appears to influence the level’s spatial behavior, guiding wanderers toward the field. == '''Nostalgic Forest (Safe Zone)''' == [[File:Forest_Zone-Winnie_the_Pooh_house.jpg|center|thumb|'''An emblematic habitation area in the Forest Zone.''']]The next major zone of Level 876 is a '''nostalgic forest''' that blends natural scenery with stylized elements reminiscent of 1940s animated films. The trees are tall and slightly exaggerated in shape, with soft outlines and warm coloration that give the entire area a hand‑painted appearance. The air carries a faint sweetness, and the lighting remains consistently golden, as though frozen in a perpetual late afternoon. The forest contains numerous environmental features, including beehives, clusters of flowering plants, wooden logs, and several chopped‑down tree stumps that serve as seating. Scattered throughout the area are small '''tree‑village dwellings''', each carved directly into the trunks of large trees. These homes contain jars of honey that can be collected, along with furniture constructed from bamboo and covered with patchwork quilts. The interiors feel cozy and lived‑in, though no residents have ever been observed. At the center of the village lies a communal clearing with a campfire pit and a simple navigation map etched into a wooden board. The map appears to depict the surrounding forest trails, though its accuracy varies between visits. Wanderers often use this clearing as a resting point before continuing deeper into the zone. While generally considered safe, the forest contains mild hazards. Beehives may attract territorial bees, and certain plants exhibit unpredictable behavior when disturbed. These dangers are minimal compared to the level’s Unsafe Zones, but wanderers should remain cautious when interacting with wildlife. The Nostalgic Forest can be accessed from multiple areas within Level 876, most commonly through transitional paths leading from the Wheat Trail Field or other nature‑themed zones.[[File:Olden_School_(2).webp|center|thumb|300x300px|
An image of '''The Olden School'''.
]] == '''Olden School (Unsafe)''' == The '''Olden School''' is the third major zone of Level 876, manifesting as a desaturated, old‑fashioned educational institution with architecture and furnishings reminiscent of the late 1800s. The building’s interior is dim, illuminated only by natural light filtering through tall windows. Despite the absence of artificial lighting, the rooms appear strangely bright, as though the sunlight is being amplified by the environment itself. The floors are made of worn, scratched wood that creaks underfoot. Classrooms contain neatly arranged rows of desks, each accompanied by stacks of faded textbooks, unfinished assignments, and chalk‑covered slates. The air carries a faint mixture of chalk dust, drying paint, and aged paper. These sensory details intensify the longer a wanderer remains inside, contributing to a growing sense of unease. Hallways are significantly darker than the classrooms, lit only by narrow beams of natural light. Their layouts are irregular and disorienting, with lockers and doors placed in inconsistent patterns that offer no reliable navigation. Some corridors loop unexpectedly, while others terminate abruptly. An exit door at the end of one hallway leads back outside, where a teacher‑like figure stands silently, observing wanderers without interacting. Unstable spatial phenomena occur within the school’s passageways. Small vortexes occasionally open in the air, pulling wanderers a short distance before depositing them elsewhere within the building. These distortions appear without warning and vanish just as quickly. Accessing the Olden School requires locating a dirt road lined with black, vintage '''1920s automobiles'''. Following this road will lead directly to the school’s entrance. The zone can be reached from either the '''Midzone''' or the '''Wheat Trail Field''', depending on the wanderer’s path through Level 876. == '''Subway and Train Car (Unsafe)''' == [[File:SUBWAY_TRAIN_CAR.webp|center|thumb|267x267px|An image of the train passenger car.]]The fourth zone of Level 876 is known as the '''Subway and Train Car''', a refined 1800s‑style train depot illuminated by warm natural light. The station features ornate wooden benches, brass fixtures, and a single matte‑black train that idles on the tracks. The train is capable of transporting passengers to various destinations within Level 876 and, on rare occasions, to other levels. Although the train clearly follows a set of rules, the exact requirement for boarding remains unknown. Wanderers occasionally encounter familiar humans from reality within this zone. These individuals appear dressed in period‑appropriate 1800s clothing and behave as ordinary passengers, boarding the train without acknowledging the wanderer’s presence. Their origin is undetermined. The '''Conductor''' oversees the depot and provides food to passengers preparing for extended travel. His behavior is calm and methodical, and he rarely speaks unless approached directly. The train itself can travel to designated '''Midzone''', '''Safe Zone''', or '''Unsafe Zone''' stops, depending on the route it selects. Despite its orderly appearance, the depot is classified as an '''Unsafe Zone''' due to the presence of the '''Voices of Rot'''. These entities can enter the station and influence passengers, causing behavioral distortions. Affected individuals do not immediately attack wanderers; instead, they become hostile only when provoked or approached aggressively. Their presence creates a subtle tension throughout the depot. This zone can be accessed from the '''Olden School''' by following a narrow road leading east. The transition is seamless, with the school’s desaturated hallways giving way to the polished wooden floors of the depot. == '''Your Home (Unsafe)''' == [[File:YourHomeUnsafe.jpg|center|thumb|An entrance to the wanderer's abandoned home modified by the 1800s era.]]The fifth zone of Level 876 is known as '''Your Home''', an area that manifests as the wanderer’s personal residence from reality. The structure is immediately recognizable, retaining the layout, objects, and furnishings associated with the wanderer’s actual home. However, the interior is altered to resemble an '''1800s mid‑Victorian aesthetic''', featuring period‑appropriate wallpaper, wooden furniture, and muted color palettes. Despite these changes, wanderers consistently identify the residence as unmistakably their own. At first glance, the house appears clean and well‑maintained, lacking dust, mold, or signs of age. Closer inspection reveals inconsistencies: certain rooms may be relocated, merged, or entirely missing, while hallways occasionally lead to unfamiliar spaces. These architectural shifts occur without sound or warning, contributing to the zone’s unstable nature. Although the environment may seem comforting, '''Your Home is classified as an Unsafe Zone'''. Disembodied voices are frequently reported within the residence, often triggered when wanderers attempt to interact with objects tied to their past lives—such as personal belongings, photographs, or familiar entertainment devices. These voices vary in tone and clarity, sometimes resembling distant conversations, reprimands, or indistinct murmuring. Their presence is considered one of the primary hazards of this zone. Over time, the house begins to decay. The deterioration resembles a slow timelapse: mold spreads across wallpaper, wooden surfaces rot, and furnishings collapse under their own weight. In rare cases, wanderers have described their home briefly shifting into a '''postmodern appearance''' before reverting to its Victorian form and undergoing rapid decomposition. This decay appears to respond to emotional states, memory triggers, or prolonged occupancy. A narrow road outside the residence bears a street name familiar to the wanderer, reinforcing the illusion of personal connection. Following this road will always lead directly to the manifestation of the wanderer’s home, regardless of the surrounding terrain.[[File:Level_876_home_bedroom_area.jpg|center|thumb|238x238px|A bedroom found in the same residence of Level 876.]] == '''Recess Zone (Safe)''' == The '''Recess Zone''' is the eighth major area of Level 876 and one of the few regions that shows no signs of decay. It manifests as a compact, brightly colored playground situated in the center of an open field of wheat grass. The wheat resembles that of '''Level 10''', swaying gently in the wind and forming a natural boundary around the play area. The playground equipment includes red slides, yellow monkey bars, a metal climbing structure, and a multicolored swingset painted in shades of green, pink, and red. Wood chips cover the ground beneath the equipment, producing soft crunching sounds when stepped on. Despite its cheerful appearance, the zone remains completely silent aside from ambient wind, creating an uncanny contrast between its vibrant colors and its stillness. The Recess Zone can be accessed from the '''Olden School''' or the '''Daycare''', often appearing as an outdoor extension of those areas. It may also manifest spontaneously when wanderers travel through various other zones, particularly when they are experiencing strong childhood memories or seeking a familiar, comforting environment. The zone’s refusal to decay suggests that it is stabilized by nostalgic associations rather than environmental conditions. Although classified as a safe zone, the Recess Zone’s isolation and stillness can be disorienting. Wanderers report a subtle emotional pull toward the playground equipment, though interacting with it has not produced any known hazards. The surrounding wheat field loops seamlessly, ensuring that wanderers who leave the playground eventually return to the same central clearing. == '''Daycare Zone (Safe)''' == The ninth zone is the Day Care Zone; it resembles one's first time going to school ever. The place looks just how one used to remember it: white walls, colorful rugs, and toys. It can be found by simply walking around, and it's a place a wanderer can engage with blocks and toys of all kinds, unwind and discover. There are no entities present in this area, just an empty daycare from the days of childhood. This daycare is one of the only zones in this level that isn't an early 1800s-era place; the rest of the world here is. People can access this level only from the Unsafe Zone, which is usually risky, The Unsafe Zone is where no color is present, but there is a small and rare chance you could enter here from the '''Wheat Trail Field.''' == '''Photo Zones''' == The '''Photo Zones''' are a collection of memory‑based sub‑areas within Level 876 that can be accessed through physical photographs found inside the '''Your Home''' zone or other residential‑style locations. These photographs depict ordinary places—parks, rooms, streets, landscapes—but their origins and the method by which they were taken remain unknown. To enter a Photo Zone, a wanderer must press and hold their fingers against the surface of a photograph. After several seconds, the image begins to distort, and the wanderer is transported directly into a zone that matches the scene shown in the photo. The resulting environment is an exact recreation of the depicted location, including lighting, weather, and spatial layout, though often with subtle desaturation or period‑appropriate styling consistent with Level 876’s aesthetic. Photo Zones vary in size and stability. Some are small, containing only a single room or outdoor clearing, while others expand into larger environments that loop or connect back to other zones. These areas typically retain the safe‑zone properties of Level 876 unless the photograph itself contains signs of decay, damage, or emotional significance. In such cases, the recreated zone may exhibit Unsafe Zone traits, including environmental rot or auditory anomalies. The logic behind how these photographs were captured—and why they possess the ability to transport wanderers—is undetermined. No camera or recording device within Level 876 has been shown to produce new functional Photo Zones, suggesting that the images originate from an unknown external source or from the wanderer’s own memories. == ''
Anomalies
'' == The principle of the future is completely absent from '''Level 876'''. Plants normally don’t grow in the unsafe zones. Level 876's threshold bears influences from various Backrooms levels, including '''[[Level 0]] (The Lobby), [[Level 10]] (The Bumper Crop), [[Level 130]] , and [[Level 26]] (Otherworldly)'''. Checking the time in unsafe and false memory zones causes visual disturbances and temporary eye discomfort, while doing so in safe zones can help determine the area's safety. Nobody is aware of which memories are listed as false memory zones == ''Entities'' == === '''Voices of Rot''' === The Voices of Rot, or the "V.O.R.," are disembodied auditory entities that induce intrusive, guilt‑focused hallucinations. They typically reference past mistakes, personal habits, or emotionally sensitive memories, causing psychological distress and accelerating environmental decay within the affected zone. Exposure to the Voices of Rot may result in sudden dermatological symptoms, including rashes, blistering, and accelerated wrinkling of the skin. These effects are temporary and can be treated with standard Backrooms medical supplies such as almond water, Firesalt mixtures, or other restorative items. In severe cases, the Voices of Rot can manifest visual projections of the wanderer performing distressing or self‑critical actions. These manifestations appear in real time and behave independently, functioning as psychological constructs rather than physical entities. === '''Personnel''' === This type of entity is purely humanoid and resembles normal human anatomy and can consist of teachers, ministers, friends who misled you, or just people you were familiar with. In unsafe zones, they become hostile or extremely talkative about a particular topic. Avoid prolonged interaction. In safe zones, they can be helpful and offer good guidance. In unsafe zones, if a supernatural occurrence or effect happens to them, their eyes will turn black and they will try to discover ways to kill the wanderer. === '''Animals''' === Animals that appear in the safe zones of Level 876—most commonly within the Wheat Trail Field—behave similarly to their Frontrooms counterparts. They remain passive, avoid wanderers, and exhibit normal movement patterns. However, when these animals cross into an '''Unsafe Zone,''' their behavior changes abruptly. Creatures exposed to Unsafe Zone conditions become hostile and unpredictable. Their eyes turn completely black, and they display heightened aggression, territorial behavior, and an unusual resistance to fear stimuli. These changes occur rapidly and appear to be directly linked to the environmental decay present in '''Unsafe Zones.''' Wanderers are advised to avoid approaching any animal once these symptoms are observed. === '''The Conductor''' === A powerful entity known as '''The Conductor''' is believed to regulate portions of Level 876 through music. He manifests as a colossal humanoid figure, estimated to stand over '''100 feet tall''', often seen towering above the clouds. The Conductor wears a dark bowler hat and is typically depicted playing a piano suspended within the cloud layer. He also carries a violin, though no wanderer has ever reported hearing it played. The Conductor is not omnipresent and appears only in select areas of the level, most commonly within the '''Wheat Trail Field''' safe zone. His presence is accompanied by soft, distant piano melodies that seem to influence the emotional tone of the environment. These melodies have been described as calming, nostalgic, or subtly melancholic. No hostile behavior has ever been observed from the Conductor, and wanderers generally regard him as a stabilizing force within Level 876. == ''Group Outposts'' == === Timely Boys === [[File:Mac_and_Jade.webp|center|frame]]A duo of survivors and college-aged youngsters known as 22-year-old '''Mack Chamberlain''' and 20-year-old '''Jaeden Tristopher''' have their own “outpost” somewhere in the '''Wheat Trail Field''', which looks like a small stone cottage residence. They are friendly and offer assistance to other wanderers. They tend to disappear from their outpost at times, mainly to keep track of Level 876 further, to gather nourishment or necessities, or even to discover other levels in the Backrooms, but not for too long. The only technology the outpost uses is Jaeden's and Mack's laptop computers, which they brought from another level or from their residence in the Frontrooms. The other technology they have is primitive and made from sticks, stones, and the environment of Level 876, such as munitions and catapults. Mack and Jaeden also own and exchange '''Firesalt,''' '''[[Object 1|Almond Water]];''' common food items like pizza, chicken, and energy drinks, and one of each exclusive and cosmic Backrooms item. Something always compels the two to return to Level 896 and to attend their outpost despite them not being as structurally advanced as popular groups in the Backrooms. They are usually dependable and strive to do their maximum not to succumb abruptly in the level so they can still make sense of Level 876 and meet new visitors. This outpost is free for everyone to join; however, no one has ever joined. === '''MEG Group''' === The '''Major Explorer Group (M.E.G.)''' maintains a small research outpost within the Midzone of Level 876. The structure appears to have been assembled from local materials, though the exact method used to construct it remains unclear. The outpost contains limited supplies, basic survival equipment, and several computer terminals used for documenting environmental changes and cataloging anomalies observed throughout the level. The M.E.G.’s primary objectives in this region include long‑term study of Level 876’s memory‑coded phenomena, monitoring the spread of Unsafe Zone anomalies, and providing minimal assistance to wanderers who arrive in the Midzone. Due to the level’s unpredictable behavior, the outpost operates with a reduced staff and maintains only essential functions.
Note by a Wanderer ----
'''Date:''' August 10th, 2013 '''Recorded by:''' Jaeden T. (Timely Boyz Outpost)
Level 876 doesn’t feel like a normal Backrooms level. The moment Mack and I arrived, the air felt heavier — not dangerous, just old. The Midzone pulled us forward in a straight line, almost like the place wanted us to see something. The Wheat Trail Field was the first area we reached. The sky was bright, but the light didn’t behave correctly. Shadows stretched in ways I couldn’t explain. The field itself was calm, but every few minutes I heard faint cracking sounds coming from somewhere behind us. Mack didn’t hear them. We built our outpost in a stone cottage we found near the trail. It’s small, but stable. We store food, journals, and whatever tools we can carry. The Voices of Rot haven’t reached this area yet, but we’ve seen signs of their influence in the Unsafe Zones — warped soil, brown sky patches, and that strange pressure in the air. If anyone reaches Level 876, stay in the safe zones until you understand the terrain. The level isn’t hostile, but it reacts to you. It remembers you. And if you’re not careful, it will show you things you weren’t ready to see.
== ''Entrances and Exits'' == ==== '''Old Data (Deprecated Information)''' ==== Earlier documentation on Level 876 was limited, and its entrance points were once considered entirely unknown. Early wanderer reports suggested that access to the level might be tied to personal memories or emotional triggers, particularly those associated with guilt, nostalgia, or unresolved experiences. Several outdated theories attempted to explain potential escape methods. Some wanderers believed that focusing on imagery from '''Level 10''' while standing in the '''Recess Zone''' could create a transition out of Level 876. Others claimed that playing specific piano notes in the '''Wheat Trail Field''' might open an exit pathway. None of these methods have been verified, and they are now regarded as historical speculation rather than reliable procedures. '''This information is preserved for archival purposes only.''' === '''Confirmed Data: Verified Exits''' === Recent documentation from the '''Major Explorer Group (M.E.G.)''' and independent explorers has confirmed the existence of several reliable exit points within Level 876. These exits have been observed consistently across multiple expeditions and are now recognized as guaranteed methods of leaving the level under normal conditions.
=== '''Entry:''' === * '''[[Level 18]]''': As of '''November 6th, 2015''', Level 18 is the first confirmed entrance to Level 876. Wanderers may enter by locating an '''out‑of‑place exit''' within any old home environment resembling the '''18th–19th century'''. The exit must visually match the era of the surrounding level. Additionally, entering a '''dark hallway''' within Level 18’s daycare section and walking for approximately '''one minute''' will transport the wanderer to Level 876. * '''[[Level 1000]]''' has a way for wanderers to enter Level 876; locating the source of old timey music can send you here to Level 876. * Clipping into a painting depicting the '''Wheat Trail Field''' in '''[[Level 28]],''' will transport one to Level '''876'''. === '''Exits:''' === * '''[[Level 10|To Level 10:]]''' Focusing intently on Level 10 while standing within the '''Recess Zone''' will shift the wanderer there. '''[[Level 18|To Level 18]]:''' Entering any '''dark hallway''' connected to this level’s daycare structures will return the wanderer to '''Level 18'''. '''[[Level 30|To Level 30]]:''' Viewing a large poster of an '''indoor water park''' (dated between the 20th and 21st century) will transport the wanderer to '''Level 30'''. '''From [[Level 93]] to Level 876:''' Looking through a '''frosted, cold apartment window''' in Level 93 can send a wanderer to Level 876, provided the window reveals a '''snowy landscape''' with a '''pink, eerie horizon'''. {{User:TheBackroomsMapMaker/Template:BoldedRedirect|Prev=875|Next=877}} {{CSS|content=.page__main, .main-container, #content, .page-content { background: #d8c8a8 !important;
